summary: Linked the Kraid stage module by switching src/melee/gr/grkraid.c from NonMatching to Matching and adjusting code plus symbol metadata so data, rodata, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 layout matched. The PR body notes that the remaining blockers were section placement and padding issues for strings and small data; these were fixed before merge, including a described janky hack for a 4-byte offset.
what_changed: configure.py changed the build object entry for melee/gr/grkraid.c from Object(NonMatching, "melee/gr/grkraid.c") to Object(Matching, "melee/gr/grkraid.c"), linking the module as matching.

src/melee/gr/grkraid.c had include cleanup and ordering changes, replacing broad or local includes such as types.h, platform.h, forward.h, and several baselib/gr headers with the includes needed for the matched source, including gr/forward.h and mn/forward.h.

A file-scope const Vec grKr_803B8278 = { 0.0f, 0.0f, 0.0f } was added and grKraid_801FE440 now initializes Vec pos from that symbol instead of an inline local zero vector, matching the rodata symbol layout.

The helper getMapRotation was removed/inlined into grKraid_801FE6D8. The rewritten code introduces local max and min temporaries, computes rot in two statements, and includes a self-assignment to grKr_804D6A08->map_rot_spd_min, apparently to shape generated code.

grKraid_801FE818 was rewritten slightly to alter local-variable ordering and initialization, including fVar1 = (fVar3 = 0.0f), removing repeated fVar3 = fVar1 assignments, changing an int variable to s8 iVar, and assigning gp->gv.kraid2.x2 and x1 through a casted temporary.

config/GALE01/symbols.txt was updated for grkraid-related data layout: several grKr symbols became local @NNN symbols, sizes were corrected, and alignments were added for adjacent grRc symbols to preserve section boundaries and padding.

The data symbol at 0x803E4D64 was represented as a local string @171 of size 0xA, addressing the PR body's noted issue where a string needed to be placed with an offset instead of starting at an undesired location.
sdata, sbss, and sdata2 grKr entries were retagged from global grKr_* names to local @NNN labels or local scope where appropriate, while some following grRc entries gained align:8 metadata.
smart_moves: Solved matching as a combined source-code and symbol-layout problem rather than only changing C code; the PR adjusts config/GALE01/symbols.txt across rodata, data, sdata, sbss, and sdata2 to make the linker reproduce the original layout.

Used a file-scope const Vec for the zero vector in grKraid_801FE440, which forces the zero vector into rodata instead of leaving it as a stack/local initializer, aligning with the symbol table change for grKr_803B8278.

Converted several compiler-sensitive expressions into temporaries in grKraid_801FE6D8 and grKraid_801FE818, a common matching tactic for influencing register allocation, expression ordering, and stores without changing apparent runtime behavior.

Preserved anonymous/local constants as @NNN symbols in symbols.txt instead of over-naming them as global grKr_* symbols, which better represents compiler-emitted local literals and avoids disturbing nearby modules.
Added explicit align metadata to adjacent symbols such as grRc_803B8288, grRc_803E4DA8, and grRc_804D4780 to account for required padding when grkraid data sizes changed.
decomp_lessons: When linking a stage file, matching can depend on accurate section metadata for neighboring symbols, not just the target file's functions. Shrinking or localizing one grKr object may require adding align metadata to the following grRc object.

If a string or object must appear at a nontrivial offset inside a data cluster, representing surrounding items as local @NNN labels with exact sizes may be more effective than forcing all entries to named globals.
Small zero vectors used in code may need to be materialized as const rodata symbols to match original layout and instruction selection.
Inlining or de-inlining helpers can affect matching. Here, replacing getMapRotation with explicit local temporaries inside grKraid_801FE6D8 was part of the matching diff.

Seemingly redundant operations, such as assigning a struct field to itself, can be intentional codegen anchors in decomp work. Treat them as matching artifacts unless later evidence proves they are removable.
For small integer fields, matching may require narrowing casts and shared temporaries, as seen with assigning kraid2.x2 and kraid2.x1 through an s8 temporary derived from a float-zero value.
naming_conventions: Stage Kraid symbols use the grKr prefix for named globals, e.g. grKr_803B8278, grKr_803E4C78, and grKr_804D6A08.
Anonymous compiler-emitted constants and strings are represented as local @NNN labels in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, e.g. @147, @170, @171, @198, @287, @224, and @431.

Neighboring stage prefixes remain distinct in symbols.txt, such as grRc for Rainbow Cruise, grI2 for Icicle Mountain/Inishie-related entries, and grYt for Yoshi's Story/Yorster entries; align changes to neighboring symbols should preserve those prefixes.
Function names in the source keep the grKraid_80xxxxxx address-style convention, e.g. grKraid_801FE440, grKraid_801FE6D8, and grKraid_801FE818.
Ground object parameters are typed as Ground_GObj* and local ground data is accessed with GET_GROUND(gobj), consistent with surrounding ground/stage code.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed a local Vec zero initializer into a global const Vec load to match rodata use.
Removed a standalone getMapRotation helper and expanded its arithmetic at the call site to affect emitted code shape.
Split arithmetic into explicit max, min, and rot temporaries: max = ..., min = ..., rot = (max / min) / 132.0f; rot = rot * min.
Inserted a no-op-looking field self-assignment to influence code generation: grKr_804D6A08->map_rot_spd_min = grKr_804D6A08->map_rot_spd_min.
Adjusted local declaration order and types in grKraid_801FE818, including changing an integer temporary to s8 and initializing two byte fields through the same narrowed value.
Used symbols.txt size, scope, local-label, and align annotations to force exact placement of data/rodata/sdata/sdata2/sbss objects.
Marked grkraid sdata2 float/double constants as local @NNN entries rather than global grKr_* symbols, matching compiler-local literal behavior.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided context.
The PR body records that remaining section placement and padding problems were resolved with help from 桜 on Discord.
The author explicitly called the final 4-byte offset solution a janky hack, suggesting the fix was pragmatic and matching-focused rather than a clean semantic refactor.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs that link ground stage files by converting Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching).
Search for PRs that use const Vec globals to force rodata placement of zero vectors.
Search symbols.txt history for data string offset fixes involving local @NNN labels and align:8 metadata.
Look for other matching hacks involving self-assignment to a struct field.
Compare grkraid.c matching tactics with later cleanup PRs to see whether the janky 4-byte offset hack was replaced.
